perturbation.original_text: Integrity will obtain master lease and print agreements with all third party owners of masters embodied in the Product at its own expense. Mechanical rates will not exceed [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the then-current statutory rate, and Print licenses will not exceed a prorated portion of [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the retail selling price, Integrity will also manufacture the Product and sell to TL its requirements for resale.
perturbation.changed_text: Integrity will endeavor to obtain master lease and print agreements with all third party owners of masters embodied in the Product. Best effort will be made to not exceed [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the then-current statutory rate for Mechanical rates, and a reasonable effort will be made to keep Print licenses at a prorated portion of [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the retail selling price. Integrity will also manufacture the Product and sell to TL its requirements for resale.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Integrity will obtain' to 'Integrity will endeavor to obtain' and adding the qualifying language 'Best effort will be made' and 'reasonable effort will be made', we weaken the obligation on Integrity to secure the best possible rates, which creates an in-text contradiction in the agreement. It contradicts section 9, where both parties 'warrant, represents, covenants and agrees that it has the right and power to enter into this Agreement, to grant the rights herein granted by it, and to perform the services agreed to be performed by it hereunder'. The modification in section 1c provides Integrity with loopholes that undermine this warranty, as they are only expected to 'endeavor to obtain' rights and make a 'reasonable effort' to control costs, versus a more definitive commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereto warrants, represents, covenants and agrees that it has the right and power to enter into this Agreement, to grant the rights herein granted by it, and to perform the services agreed to be performed by it hereunder, and that no materials, ideas or other properties furnished or designated by it is subject to any restriction whatsoever, or is violative of the rights of any person, firm or corporation, including, without limitation, contract rights, copyrights and rights of privacy.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposed a clear obligation on Integrity to 'obtain' agreements at specific rates. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'endeavor to obtain' agreements with 'best effort' and 'reasonable effort' clauses. This directly contradicts the 'warrants, represents, covenants and agrees' language in the contradicted text, which affirms Integrity's right and power to perform services. The modified text introduces ambiguity and potential loopholes that undermine the original warranty, creating a strong in-text contradiction because Integrity's 'right and power' to perform services is now qualified by 'endeavor' and 'reasonable effort'.

perturbation.original_text: TL may purchase from Integrity limited quantities of the Product for its promotional use, at the Manufacturing cost set forth in paragraph 3(a) above, provided the quantity of such purchases does not exceed seven percent (7%) of the total royalty bearing units of such Product title purchased by TL, TL warrants that any units so purchased, whether or not labeled "promotional only" or cut-out, will be given away for purposes of promotion of the Products, and will not be sold.
perturbation.changed_text: TL may purchase from Integrity limited quantities of the Product for its promotional use, at a price to be determined, but not to exceed the Manufacturing cost set forth in paragraph 3(a) above. The quantity of such purchases should generally not exceed seven percent (7%) of the total royalty bearing units of such Product title purchased by TL. TL intends that any units so purchased, whether or not labeled "promotional only" or cut-out, will be given away for purposes of promotion of the Products, and will not be sold.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'at the Manufacturing cost' to 'at a price to be determined, but not to exceed the Manufacturing cost' and replacing 'does not exceed' with 'should generally not exceed', we introduce ambiguity around the price TL will pay and the quantity they can purchase. Also changing 'TL warrants that any units...will be given away' to 'TL intends that any units...will be given away' weakens the commitment of TL, creating an in-text contradiction. This weakens TL's obligation to only use them for promotional purposes. This contradicts section 2a, where TL will have exclusive rights to promote and sell the product through certain distribution channels, but using the promotional quantities as avenues for general market retail.  This creates an internal contradiction if TL uses promotional units to indirectly sell product through distribution channels outside those intended for promotional use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TL will hold exclusive worldwide rights to promote and sell the product through the following distribution channels: Outgoing telemarketing, General Market retail, and General Market catalogs, and exclusive rights within the United States for Television Direct response. TL will have Internet and e-commerce rights with respect to its own web site and other General Market e-commerce. In addition, TL may cross-sell the products to its internal list by inserts placed in mailings for other TL products and services (but excluding direct mail campaigns for the Product.) TL may obtain television and/or direct response rights in certain international territories upon Integrity's agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens obligations, creating a strong contradiction. Specifically, 'at a price to be determined, but not to exceed the Manufacturing cost' and 'should generally not exceed seven percent (7%)' introduces uncertainty. Most importantly, the shift from 'TL warrants that any units...will be given away' to 'TL intends that any units...will be given away' significantly weakens TL's commitment, directly contradicting the original intent and creating a clear avenue for using promotional units for general retail. This conflicts with the contradicted text, which grants TL exclusive rights to sell through specific channels, because now TL could use the promotional units for avenues outside those intended for promotional use. This introduces ambiguity where the original text expected certainty and directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section, this falls under the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Accounting statements and, if applicable, royalty payments for products sold will be rendered by each party quarterly within sixty (60) days after the expiration of each calendar quarter. Only products which have been paid or credited to the account of the seller shall be deemed sold.
perturbation.changed_text: Accounting statements and, if applicable, royalty payments for products sold will ideally be rendered by each party quarterly, with a target of within sixty (60) days after the expiration of each calendar quarter. Products will generally be deemed sold once they have been shipped, but reconciliation will occur when payments are received.
perturbation.explanation: The original section specifies clear deadlines and conditions for accounting and royalty payments. By adding 'ideally be rendered' and 'with a target of', the timeline becomes less definitive. Changing 'Only products which have been paid or credited to the account of the seller shall be deemed sold' to 'Products will generally be deemed sold once they have been shipped, but reconciliation will occur when payments are received' introduces a discrepancy between when a product is 'deemed sold' for accounting purposes and when payment is actually received. This can have a cascading effect on many parts of the agreement, mainly section 5 which states 'On sales of products pursuant to this Agreement (less any returns) TL will pay to Integrity royalties in the amount of [**]/1/ ($[**]/1/) for each and every unit of the product sold by it pursuant to this Agreement'. This creates confusion by introducing ambiguity about when royalties are earned, as it now depends on the interpretation of 'sold', if it's shipped or paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: On sales of products pursuant to this Agreement (less any returns) TL will pay to Integrity royalties in the amount of [**]/1/ ($[**]/1/) for each and every unit of the product sold by it pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that royalty payments are based on products that have been paid or credited. The changed text shifts this to royalty payments being generally triggered by shipment but requiring reconciliation upon payment. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states royalties are paid 'on sales of products', since the definition of 'sold' has changed from payment received to shipped. This creates a contradiction as to when the royalty obligation arises: upon shipment or upon payment, which clearly impacts the amount and timing of payments. As the definition of sold is changed, it directly leads to the inconsistency outlined in the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
